name: audit-hub-webapp
description: Single-page React app for the Audit Evidence Hub. One codebase whose navigation and screens adapt to the signed-in user's role — Compliance Admin (control authoring, assignment, full visibility), Team Manager (their team's controls, evidence submission with image upload), Auditor (assigned-to-them review queue, approve/reject). Talks only to audit-hub-api for data; signs users in via the shared user-auth OIDC dependency.

dependencies.name: audit-hub-api
dependencies.description: Backend for all control, evidence, and review data; called with the caller's bearer token.

dependencies.name: user-auth
dependencies.description: End-user sign-in for all three roles via the organization's identity provider; role is read from the id_token's groups claim.

componentAgentInstructions: OIDC Authorization Code + PKCE against the platform IDP using oidc-client-ts. Read OIDC config from window._env_.USER_AUTH_* and the API URL from window._env_.AUDIT_HUB_API_URL — typed via src/env.ts, throw on any missing key. Compute redirect_uri = window.location.origin + import.meta.env.BASE_URL + 'callback' and serve the callback route under that base subpath. Attach Authorization: Bearer <access_token> to every audit-hub-api call. Read the caller's role from user.profile.groups (Compliance Admin / Team Manager / Auditor) and render the matching shell/navigation; do not hardcode role logic beyond that claim. DO NOT write a .env file or read import.meta.env.VITE_*. DO NOT use envsubst, /etc/nginx/templates/, or a custom nginx entrypoint — stock nginx:alpine serves the static bundle + env-config.js. Set vite.config.ts base to the deploy subpath and index.html's env-config.js script tag to a relative ./env-config.js path.
